canonical_serialization_note: K2 test computes SHA-256 hex over the canonical_serialization UTF-8 bytes via CommonCrypto CC_SHA256; asserts equal to expected_ledger_digest_hex.
expected_stale_mutation_source: SessionLifecycleRecoveryCoordinator.oldGenerationStaleCallbackCount() after schedule replay; incremented by delivering a lifecycle event carrying the pre-rotation generation to RecoveryCoordinator.apply(_:authority:) after rotate. The child registry's own stale counter is separate and covers pre-fence late callbacks referencing still-known children.

test_oracle_independence_note: K2 test hard-codes expected_ledger_digest_hex above; independently computes SHA-256 in-test via CommonCrypto over canonical_serialization and asserts both against the fixture value and against a K2-driven schedule re-serialization. K2 implementation code MUST NOT expose the digest as a property that the test would then read back to itself.
